content: We can adapt the scalable shared memory architecture design for multi-agent active learning to build a hierarchical agentic search and memory retrieval (ASMR) system, where each active learning agent contributes newly annotated high-value data to a shared global retention-backed memory index that ASMR queries dynamically. This combined framework lets ASMR prioritize searching over the most information-dense unlabeled data points first, rather than conducting brute-force search over all stored memory.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
trigger_topic: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
